title: Verilator creating unknown C++ symbols due to a misplaced semi-colon
body:
---


Author Name: **Michael Berman**
Original Redmine Issue: 976 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2015-10-02


---

In the simple Verilog module provided below, if a semi-colon is mistakenly placed at the end of the line declaring the for loop, Verilator will not produce a lint error or warning, but will create symbols based on the genvar that are never declared within the verilated C++ code.
